channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Sony Reportedly Making Single Player Games Exclusive To PlayStation Again
item.content:encoded:
After months of speculation, a new report from Bloomberg's Jason Schrier tells us that Sony has indeed changed their multi-platform plans for PlayStation games. 

During a Monday morning meeting PlayStation studio business CEO Hermen Hulst explained the new plan was that their prestige narrative single-player games would no longer be ported to other platforms like they had been for the last few years. This wouldn't impact multiplayer-first and online games such as Marathon, and Marvel Tokon which will continue to release across multiple platforms to get a larger playerbase to make them more sustainable.

This undoes a change that began during 2020 when Horizon Zero Dawn kicked off an accelerating trend of single-player games getting ported onto PC. Other titles included God of War, Marvel's Spider-Man, and Uncharted: Legacy of Thieves Collection, but diminishing returns post-2022 may be part of the reason for the change. A simple example is that the 2022 release of Spider-Man Remastered had a Steam peak player count of over 66 thousand at launch, while it's sequel Marvel's Spider-Man 2 released in 2025 topped out at just over 28k. All of these single player titles released after a period of time exclusive to the PlayStation console, but there was never any consistent cadence to it.

Externally developed games published by PlayStation may continue to arrive on PC. Per Schrier's report, at the very least Death Stranding 2 and Kena: Scars of Kosmora will also release on PC despite not being traditional 'online' titles. 

At the same time we're seeing this the console market is undergoing changes with Xbox talking about their next device potentially running Windows, which would have allowed it to run PC native PlayStation titles, and Valve's revamped Steam Machine line is looking at challenging in the living room as well. There have also been the increases in prices brought on due to the chip shortage that makes the PlayStation 5 even more of a luxury good than it was before.

 Sony has been making changes as well to its online game situation as Marathon has struggled some out of the game. They've also cancelled a number of games and the recently released Ghost of Yotei: Legends multiplayer mode is ending adding new content after only one month.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
